codeReview.summary: The product-lock backend wiring is correctly implemented in src/worker.ts. Six helper functions and the ProductLock type are imported, data handlers are registered for product-lock and product-locks, and action handlers are registered for acquire-product-lock, release-product-lock, and check-merge-conflict. Typecheck and build pass. The implementation mirrors the handlers in src/autopilot/worker.ts lines 2718-2782.

issues.description: Feature description mentioned importing `findProductLockForTarget` but this function does not exist in helpers.ts. Worker correctly substituted `findBlockingLock` instead (which is the function actually used by the action handlers in autopilot/worker.ts). This substitution is correct.
`findActiveProductLock` is imported but never used in any handler. Unused imports are not ideal but do not cause functional issues.

The productLocks data handler filters by companyId but does not filter by isActive: true. The reference handlers in autopilot/worker.ts also do not filter by isActive, so this is consistent behavior, but the list may include released/inactive locks if there are any in the DB.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No shared-state gaps identified. Worker followed existing patterns: imported type from constants.js, imported helpers from helpers.js, registered data handlers in registerDataHandlers(), registered action handlers in registerActionHandlers().
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker.ts imports and registrations follow the same pattern as other data/action registrations (e.g., companyBudgets, preferenceProfile)

summary: Product-lock backend wiring is correctly implemented. The worker imported 6 helper functions and the ProductLock type, registered data handlers for product-lock/product-locks, and registered action handlers for acquire-product-lock/release-product-lock/check-merge-conflict. Typecheck and build pass. One minor deviation from feature description (findProductLockForTarget → findBlockingLock substitution) is justified because findProductLockForTarget does not exist; findBlockingLock is what the reference handlers actually use. Two minor unused items (findActiveProductLock imported but not used; productLocks data handler doesn't filter by isActive) are non-blocking.
